title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens leaves Boston College after two seasons to join the Providence Bruins on an AHL amateur tryout.
Hagens, 19, was drafted seventh overall by the Bruins in the 2025 NHL Draft and is a highly regarded prospect.
He will practice with Providence initially and is anticipated to play against Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony, a 21-year-old outfielder, is seen as a potential MVP candidate in his first full season with the Red Sox.
Right-handed pitcher Brayan Bello aims to build on his 2025 performance to solidify his role in Boston's rotation.
Marcelo Mayer, playing second base, faces durability concerns but is expected to develop into a regular franchise player.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones, Jr., Massachusetts House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection after 32 years in office.
Jones, 61, will continue representing the 20th Middlesex District in Massachusetts until January 2027.
Jones has served as Republican Minority Leader since 2002, and has been elected 17 times since 1994.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents deployed at Logan Airport in Boston as of March 23, per TSA union president Mike Gayzagian.
ICE agents were deployed to 14 busy airports amid federal government shutdown due to DHS funding issues.
DHS officers participated in a security operation at Logan Airport, unrelated to ICE deployment rumors.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, confirmed by the mayor's office.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty serves as acting chief during Suarez's leave, which began on February 26, 2026.
Suarez was promoted to fire chief in 2023 and previously served as provisional chief starting in 2021.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing outside 72 Seaport Blvd around 2:44 a.m. Sunday, according to Boston Police.
The victim suffered stab wounds to his abdomen and leg and was transported to a local hospital; his status is unknown.
No suspects have been identified or arrested, and police are actively investigating the incident.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial for assault and battery began in Essex County Superior Court in Massachusetts.
Prosecution claims Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, prompting him to shoot her in self-defense.
Defense argues Fitzsimmons intended to harm herself, not Noonan, and Noonan mischaracterized the situation to justify shooting.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted Rhode Island Division II state champions Blackstone Valley hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena.
Blackstone Valley's visit included meeting Bruins players, taking photos, and watching a practice session in Brighton.
The team recently won the state title with a quadruple-overtime win, rallying after tragedy involving a teammate's family.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed James Hudson, a 6-foot-5, 313-pound offensive tackle, previously with the Giants.
Hudson has 60 NFL games experience, starting 19, primarily at right tackle and some at left tackle.
Hudson joins Patriots' depth chart with starters Will Campbell and Morgan Moses, providing a veteran backup option.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens' pro contract with the Bruins remains unsigned after ending his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens, a top-10 Hobey Baker finalist, scored 47 points in 34 games for Boston College this past season.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating young prospects like Hagens into the team if they are ready to compete.
